messages.content: You are a Lean 4/Mathlib context-planning agent. Prepare a compact context pack for formalizing a LaTeX theorem-like source unit. The target Lean declarations aligned to the selected source unit are withheld. Return exactly one JSON object.

{
  "rules": [
    "Do not infer or reveal hidden target Lean declaration names for the selected unit.",
    "Do not write theorem/lemma Lean code in target_statement_sketch; exact API syntax belongs in needed_mathlib_context and will be hydrated by tools.",
    "Do not bundle all source parts into one conjunction unless the source unit itself requires that shape.",
    "When a source theorem states a result for several cases such as upper/lower, left/right, or an `or` hypothesis, prefer separate narrow planned_declarations for each case when the checked APIs or natural Lean statements are case-specific. Do not collapse case-specific source claims into one disjunctive theorem just to reduce task count.",
    "If a broad source unit needs a construction or auxiliary lemma before the main claim can be stated/proved, split it into ordered planned_declarations with role same_unit_helper followed by role main_claim and explicit depends_on_task_ids.",
    "Same-unit helper tasks are declarations for the later generator to create from the selected source unit, not prior context. Do not use hidden aligned/referencing Lean names for them.",
    "Use previous project declarations only if they are shown under prior_project_context.",
    "Use local_file_predecessor_declarations only as same-file helper/style context; the selected unit's aligned/referencing target declarations are omitted.",
    "Do not treat Mathlib as the only context; enumerate source/project/local/Mathlib context separately.",
    "Prefer exact Mathlib names when known; otherwise give a narrow query plus expected signature shape.",
    "Keep added context tight: prefer a few thousand tokens or less per source unit."
  ],
  "schema": {
    "units": [
      {
        "context_pack_size_risk": "low|medium|high",
        "formalization_risks": [
          "source ambiguity, missing notation, broad multi-part theorem"
        ],
        "planned_declarations": [
          {
            "depends_on_task_ids": [
              "earlier same-unit helper task ids that this task should use"
            ],
            "kind": "def|theorem|lemma|instance|notation|unknown",
            "missing_or_uncertain_context": [
              "what a second lookup round should resolve"
            ],
            "needed_mathlib_context": [
              {
                "expected_signature_or_shape": "expected type/signature/docstring",
                "name_or_query": "exact Mathlib name or narrow search query",
                "why_needed": "definition/proof/tactic support"
              }
            ],
            "needed_project_context": [
              {
                "name": "previous project declaration if provided or likely needed",
                "why_needed": "supporting theorem/definition/notation"
              }
            ],
            "needed_source_context": [
              "source labels/statements"
            ],
            "role": "main_claim|same_unit_helper",
            "source_part": "whole unit or part marker",
            "target_statement_sketch": "prose mathematical target sketch; do not write exact Lean syntax, declaration names, or guessed API argument order",
            "task_id": "unit-001-task-1"
          }
        ],
        "selector_confidence": 0.0,
        "source_focus_summary": "short summary",
        "unit_key": "unit-001"
      }
    ]
  },
  "task": "For each LaTeX source unit, decompose the source into ordered Lean declaration tasks and select tight context for each task. The context inventory must be separate: source text, previous book/source statements, previous project declarations, local file/import/style context, and selected Mathlib APIs.",
  "units": [

        "posthoc_alignment_hidden": true,
        "target_lean_available_to_selector": false

          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "lemma",
          "lean_snippet": "@[ext]\nlemma PathTuple.ext {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] {D : SimpleDigraph V} {k : ℕ}\n    {A B : kVertex V k} (pt1 pt2 : PathTuple D k A B)\n    (h : ∀ i, pt1.paths i = pt2.paths i) : pt1 = pt2 := by\n  cases pt1; cases pt2\n  simp only [PathTuple.mk.injEq]\n  ext i\n  exact h i",
          "line_range": [
            714,
            723
          ],
          "name": "PathTuple.ext",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def dyckDigraph : SimpleDigraph (ℤ × ℕ) where\n  arc u v := (v.1 = u.1 + 1 ∧ v.2 = u.2 + 1) ∨\n             (v.1 = u.1 + 1 ∧ v.2 + 1 = u.2 ∧ 0 < u.2)\n  arc_irrefl := by\n    intro ⟨x, y⟩\n    simp only [not_or, not_and]\n    constructor <;> omega",
          "line_range": [
            5222,
            5230
          ],
          "name": "dyckDigraph",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "private def translateVertex (d : ℤ) (v : ℤ × ℕ) : ℤ × ℕ := (v.1 + d, v.2)",
          "line_range": [
            5943,
            5945
          ],
          "name": "translateVertex",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "lemma",
          "lean_snippet": "private lemma dyckDigraph_arc_translate (d : ℤ) (u v : ℤ × ℕ) :\n    dyckDigraph.arc u v ↔ dyckDigraph.arc (translateVertex d u) (translateVertex d v) := by\n  unfold dyckDigraph translateVertex\n  simp only\n  constructor\n  · intro h\n    rcases h with ⟨hx, hy⟩ | ⟨hx, hy, hpos⟩\n    · left; constructor <;> omega\n    · right; constructor; omega; constructor; omega; exact hpos\n  · intro h\n    rcases h with ⟨hx, hy⟩ | ⟨hx, hy, hpos⟩\n    · left; constructor <;> omega\n    · right; refine ⟨?_, ?_, hpos⟩ <;> omega",
          "line_range": [
            5946,
            5960
          ],
          "name": "dyckDigraph_arc_translate",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def translatePath (d : ℤ) (p : SimpleDigraph.Path dyckDigraph) :\n    SimpleDigraph.Path dyckDigraph where\n  vertices := p.vertices.map (translateVertex d)\n  nonempty := by simp [p.nonempty]\n  arcs_valid := by\n    intro k hk\n    simp only [List.length_map] at hk\n    have hk' : k < p.vertices.length := Nat.lt_of_succ_lt hk\n    have hk'' : k + 1 < p.vertices.length := hk\n\n    have h1 : (p.vertices.map (translateVertex d)).get ⟨k, by simp; exact hk'⟩ =\n              translateVertex d (p.vertices.get ⟨k, hk'⟩) := by\n      simp [List.get_eq_getElem]\n    have h2 : (p.vertices.map (translateVertex d)).get ⟨k + 1, by simp; exact hk''⟩ =\n              translateVertex d (p.vertices.get ⟨k + 1, hk''⟩) := by\n      simp [List.get_eq_getElem]\n    rw [h1, h2]\n    rw [← dyckDigraph_arc_translate d]\n    exact p.arcs_valid k hk",
          "line_range": [
            5961,
            5981
          ],
          "name": "translatePath",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "lemma",
          "lean_snippet": "private lemma translatePath_start (d : ℤ) (p : SimpleDigraph.Path dyckDigraph) :\n    (translatePath d p).start = translateVertex d p.start := by\n  unfold translatePath SimpleDigraph.Path.start translateVertex\n  simp only [List.head_map]",
          "line_range": [
            5982,
            5986
          ],
          "name": "translatePath_start",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "lemma",
          "lean_snippet": "private lemma translatePath_finish (d : ℤ) (p : SimpleDigraph.Path dyckDigraph) :\n    (translatePath d p).finish = translateVertex d p.finish := by\n  unfold translatePath SimpleDigraph.Path.finish translateVertex\n  simp only [List.getLast_map]",
          "line_range": [
            5987,
            5991
          ],
          "name": "translatePath_finish",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "lemma",
          "lean_snippet": "private lemma translatePath_neg (d : ℤ) (p : SimpleDigraph.Path dyckDigraph) :\n    translatePath (-d) (translatePath d p) = p := by\n  simp only [translatePath, List.map_map]\n  congr 1\n  conv_rhs => rw [← List.map_id p.vertices]\n  congr 1\n  funext v\n  simp only [Function.comp_apply, translateVertex]\n  ext\n  · simp only [id_eq]; ring\n  · rfl",
          "line_range": [
            5992,
            6003
          ],
          "name": "translatePath_neg",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "lemma",
          "lean_snippet": "lemma translatePath_injective (d : ℤ) : Function.Injective (translatePath d) := by\n  intro p q hpq\n  have h := congrArg (translatePath (-d)) hpq\n  simp only [translatePath_neg] at h\n  exact h",
          "line_range": [
            6004,
            6012
          ],
          "name": "translatePath_injective",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "theorem",
          "lean_snippet": "theorem dyck_paths_shifted_eq_catalan (i j : ℕ) :\n    (pathsFromTo dyckDigraph dyckDigraph_pathFinite (-(2 * i : ℤ), 0) (2 * j, 0)).card =\n      catalan (i + j) := by\n\n  have equiv : (pathsFromTo dyckDigraph dyckDigraph_pathFinite (-(2 * i : ℤ), 0) (2 * j, 0)) ≃\n               (pathsFromTo dyckDigraph dyckDigraph_pathFinite (0, 0) (2 * (i + j), 0)) := by\n    refine ⟨?toFun, ?invFun, ?left_inv, ?right_inv⟩\n    case toFun =>\n      intro ⟨p, hp⟩\n      refine ⟨translatePath (2 * i) p, ?_⟩\n      rw [mem_pathsFromTo_dyck] at hp ⊢\n      constructor\n      · rw [translatePath_start, hp.1]\n        simp [translateVertex]\n      · rw [translatePath_finish, hp.2]\n        simp [translateVertex]\n        ring\n    case invFun =>\n      intro ⟨p, hp⟩\n      refine ⟨translatePath (-(2 * i : ℤ)) p, ?_⟩\n      rw [mem_pathsFromTo_dyck] at hp ⊢\n      constructor\n      · rw [translatePath_start, hp.1]\n        simp [translateVertex]\n      · rw [translatePath_finish, hp.2]\n        simp [translateVertex]\n        ring\n    case left_inv =>\n      intro ⟨p, hp⟩\n      simp only [Subtype.mk.injEq]\n      exact translatePath_neg (2 * i) p\n    case right_inv =>\n      intro ⟨p, hp⟩\n      simp only [Subtype.mk.injEq]\n      have h : translatePath (2 * ↑i) (translatePath (-(2 * ↑i)) p) = p := by\n        have := translatePath_neg (-(2 * i : ℤ)) p\n        simp only [neg_neg] at this\n        exact this\n      exact h\n  calc (pathsFromTo dyckDigraph dyckDigraph_pathFinite (-(2 * i : ℤ), 0) (2 * j, 0)).card",
          "line_range": [
            6013,
            6058
          ],
          "name": "dyck_paths_shifted_eq_catalan",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": true
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "theorem",
          "lean_snippet": "theorem catalanHankelMatrix_eq_pathWeightMatrix (k : ℕ) :\n    catalanHankelMatrix k =\n    pathWeightMatrix dyckDigraph_pathFinite dyckUnitArcWeight\n      (fun i : Fin k => (-(2 * (i : ℤ)), 0))\n      (fun j : Fin k => (2 * (j : ℤ), 0)) := by\n  ext i j\n  simp only [catalanHankelMatrix, pathWeightMatrix, Matrix.of_apply]\n  rw [pathWeightSum_dyckUnitArcWeight]\n\n  rw [← dyck_paths_shifted_eq_catalan]",
          "line_range": [
            6059,
            6078
          ],
          "name": "catalanHankelMatrix_eq_pathWeightMatrix",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "theorem",
          "lean_snippet": "theorem catalan_hankel_det_general (n : ℕ) : (catalanHankelMatrix (n + 8)).det = 1 := by\n  sorry",
          "line_range": [
            6079,
            6081
          ],
          "name": "catalan_hankel_det_general",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        }
      ],
      "previous_source_context": [
        {
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
          "source_unit": {
            "environment": "corollary",
            "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV2.tex:cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np",
            "labels": [
              "cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np",
              "eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.xA",
              "eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.yA",
              "eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.xB",
              "eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.yB",
              "eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.claim"
            ],
            "line_range": [
              130,
              163
            ],
            "parse_warnings": [],
            "part_markers": [],
            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV2.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [
              "thm.lgv.kpaths.wt"
            ],
            "source_text": "\\begin{corollary}\n[LGV lemma, nonpermutable lattice weight version]\\label{cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np}%\nConsider the setting of Theorem \\ref{thm.lgv.kpaths.wt}, but additionally\nassume that%\n\\begin{align}\n\\operatorname*{x}\\left(  A_{1}\\right)   &  \\geq\\operatorname*{x}\\left(\nA_{2}\\right)  \\geq\\cdots\\geq\\operatorname*{x}\\left(  A_{k}\\right)\n;\\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.xA}\\\\\n\\operatorname*{y}\\left(  A_{1}\\right)   &  \\leq\\operatorname*{y}\\left(\nA_{2}\\right)  \\leq\\cdots\\leq\\operatorname*{y}\\left(  A_{k}\\right)\n;\\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.yA}\\\\\n\\operatorname*{x}\\left(  B_{1}\\right)   &  \\geq\\operatorname*{x}\\left(\nB_{2}\\right)  \\geq\\cdots\\geq\\operatorname*{x}\\left(  B_{k}\\right)\n;\\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.xB}\\\\\n\\operatorname*{y}\\left(  B_{1}\\right)   &  \\leq\\operatorname*{y}\\left(\nB_{2}\\right)  \\leq\\cdots\\leq\\operatorname*{y}\\left(  B_{k}\\right)  .\n\\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.yB}%\n\\end{align}\nHere, $\\operatorname*{x}\\left(  P\\right)  $ and $\\operatorname*{y}\\left(\nP\\right)  $ denote the two coordinates of any point $P\\in\\mathbb{Z}^{2}$.\n\nThen, there are no nipats from $\\mathbf{A}$ to $\\sigma\\left(  \\mathbf{B}%\n\\right)  $ when $\\sigma\\in S_{k}$ is not the identity permutation\n$\\operatorname*{id}\\in S_{k}$. Therefore, the claim of Theorem\n\\ref{thm.lgv.kpaths.wt} simplifies to\n\\begin{align}\n&  \\det\\left(  \\left(  \\sum_{p:A_{i}\\rightarrow B_{j}}w\\left(  p\\right)\n\\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq k,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq k}\\right) \\nonumber\\\\\n&  =\\sum_{\\substack{\\mathbf{p}\\text{ is a nipat}\\\\\\text{from }\\mathbf{A}\\text{\nto }\\mathbf{B}}}w\\left(  \\mathbf{p}\\right)  .\n\\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.claim}%\n\\end{align}\n\n\\end{corollary}"
          }
        },
        {
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
          "source_unit": {
            "environment": "corollary",
            "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV2.tex:cor.lgv.binom-det-nonneg",
            "labels": [
              "cor.lgv.binom-det-nonneg"
            ],
            "line_range": [
              259,
              273
            ],
            "parse_warnings": [],
            "part_markers": [],
            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV2.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [],
            "source_text": "\\begin{corollary}\n\\label{cor.lgv.binom-det-nonneg}Let $k\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Let $a_{1},a_{2}%\n,\\ldots,a_{k}$ and $b_{1},b_{2},\\ldots,b_{k}$ be nonnegative integers such\nthat\n\\[\na_{1}\\geq a_{2}\\geq\\cdots\\geq a_{k}\\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\text{and}%\n\\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ b_{1}\\geq b_{2}\\geq\\cdots\\geq b_{k}.\n\\]\nThen,%\n\\[\n\\det\\left(  \\left(  \\dbinom{a_{i}}{b_{j}}\\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq k,\\ 1\\leq\nj\\leq k}\\right)  \\geq0.\n\\]\n\n\\end{corollary}"
          }
        }
      ],
      "prior_project_context": [
        {
          "project_declarations": [
            {
              "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
              "declared_source_labels": [
                "cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np"
              ],
              "imports": [
                "Mathlib"
              ],
              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- LGV lemma, nonpermutable lattice weight version (Corollary cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np)\n    Label: cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np\n\n    Under the sorting conditions:\n    - x(A₁) ≥ x(A₂) ≥ ⋯ ≥ x(Aₖ)\n    - y(A₁) ≤ y(A₂) ≤ ⋯ ≤ y(Aₖ)\n    - x(B₁) ≥ x(B₂) ≥ ⋯ ≥ x(Bₖ)\n    - y(B₁) ≤ y(B₂) ≤ ⋯ ≤ y(Bₖ)\n\n    The LGV formula simplifies to:\n    det((∑_{p : Aᵢ → Bⱼ} w(p))_{i,j}) = ∑_{𝐩 nipat from 𝐀 to 𝐁} w(𝐩) -/\ntheorem lgv_nonpermutable {k : ℕ} (w : ArcWeight integerLattice K)\n    (A B : kVertex (ℤ × ℤ) k)\n    (hxA : xDecreasing A) (hyA : yIncreasing A)\n    (hxB : xDecreasing B) (hyB : yIncreasing B) :\n    (pathWeightMatrix integerLattice_pathFinite w A B).det =\n      nipatWeightSum integerLattice_pathFinite w A B (Equiv.refl (Fin k))",
              "line_range": [
                4264,
                4311
              ],
              "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Determinants.LGV2",
              "name": "LGV.lgv_nonpermutable",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",

              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- First Jacobi-Trudi formula (Theorem thm.sf.jt-h)\n    Label: thm.sf.jt-h\n\n    s_{λ/μ} = det((h_{λᵢ - μⱼ - i + j})_{1 ≤ i,j ≤ M})\n\n    The proof uses the Lindström-Gessel-Viennot lemma with appropriate\n    source and target vertices in the integer lattice ℤ².\n\n    ## Proof Strategy\n\n    **Step 1: Set up the LGV framework**\n    Define source vertices Aᵢ = (μᵢ - i, 1) and target vertices Bⱼ = (λⱼ - j, N) in ℤ².\n    The path weight matrix M_{i,j} = ∑_{p : Aᵢ → Bⱼ} w(p) where w(p) is the product\n    of x_h for each east-step at height h.\n\n    **Step 2: Identify M with the Jacobi-Trudi matrix**\n    By `pathWeightSum_eq_hsymm`, M_{i,j} = h_{λⱼ - μᵢ - j + i} = h_{λⱼ - μᵢ - (j - i)}.\n    Note: The textbook uses 1-indexed notation; our Lean formalization uses 0-indexed.\n    The matrix entry (i,j) is h_{λᵢ - μⱼ - i + j} in the textbook convention.\n\n    **Step 3: Apply the LGV lemma**\n    By `lgv_nonpermutable` (Corollary cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np), since the source and\n    target vertices satisfy the sorting conditions (x-decreasing, y-increasing),\n    we have:\n      det(M) = ∑_{nipats from 𝐀 to 𝐁} w(nipat)\n    where the sum is only over non-intersecting path tuples (nipats).\n\n    **Step 4: Establish the nipat-SSYT bijection**\n    By `nipat_ssyt_bijection`, there is a weight-preserving bijection between\n    nipats from 𝐀 to 𝐁 and SSYT of skew shape λ/μ:\n    - The nipat (p₁, ..., pₖ) maps to the tableau T where row i contains\n      the heights of east-steps in path pᵢ (in weakly increasing order).\n    - Non-intersection of paths ensures column-strictness of the tableau.\n    - The weight w(nipat) = ∏ᵢ w(pᵢ) equals the monomial x^T.\n\n    **Step 5: Conclude**\n    det(M) = ∑_{nipats} w(nipat) = ∑_{SSYT T of shape λ/μ} x^T = s_{λ/μ}\n\n    ## Key Lemmas Required",

                8066
              ],

        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV2.tex",
        "referenced_labels": [],
        "source_text": "\\begin{corollary}\n\\label{cor.lgv.catalan-hankel-det-0}Let $k\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Recall the Catalan\nnumbers $c_{n}=\\dfrac{1}{n+1}\\dbinom{2n}{n}$ for all $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Then,%\n\\[\n\\det\\left(  \\left(  c_{i+j-2}\\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq k,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq k}\\right)\n=\\det\\left(\n\\begin{array}\n[c]{cccc}%\nc_{0} & c_{1} & \\cdots & c_{k-1}\\\\\nc_{1} & c_{2} & \\cdots & c_{k}\\\\\n\\vdots & \\vdots & \\ddots & \\vdots\\\\\nc_{k-1} & c_{k} & \\cdots & c_{2k-2}%\n\\end{array}\n\\right)  =1.\n\\]\n\n\\end{corollary}"
      },
      "unit_key": "unit-001"
